title: Chronic Disease Flexible Coverage Act
plain_summary: This law makes it easier for people with high deductible health plans and health savings accounts to get treatment for ongoing health problems like diabetes, heart disease, and asthma. Usually these health plans make you pay a large amount out of your own pocket before insurance starts helping. But now certain medicines and medical supplies for chronic diseases can be covered right away without having to meet that high deductible first. This includes things like insulin for diabetes, inhalers for asthma, blood pressure monitors, and cholesterol medications.
last_action_text: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance.

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Chronic Disease Flexible Coverage Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Services and Items for Chronic Conditions Treated as Preventive Care
sections.text: The Internal Revenue Service already issued guidance in Notice two thousand nineteen dash forty five about certain medical services and items for ongoing health conditions. This guidance said these services and items could be treated as preventive care. This law makes that guidance official and gives it the same legal power as if Congress had written it directly into this law. This applies to people who have high deductible health plans (health insurance where you pay more out of pocket before insurance starts paying) connected to health savings accounts (special accounts that let you save money tax free for medical expenses). This law does not change any other rules the Treasury Secretary has made or might make in the future about what counts as preventive services under the tax code.
